title: Standing Question (reconciled)
description: Reconciled data structure for entries in the unified standing-question pool. This version is the merge of a GPT-5.4-aligned draft (authored by Claude Code against GPT-5.4 feedback on 2026-04-13) and a Gemini-authored draft (overwritten the same day). Per-field design provenance and dual attribution live in the companion file `standing_question.schema.reconciliation.md`. The merge was authorized by BODY Parliament cycle 739 on 2026-04-13 with D16 Agency explicitly voting execute, after 9 of 16 domains favored Option 3 (reconciled merge) over Option 1 (restore GPT-5.4) or Option 2 (keep Gemini, document divergence). This schema is still a draft — no code currently reads or writes it — but its provenance is now cross-substrate and its design decisions are documented field-by-field in the companion reconciliation document.

id.description: Stable identifier (Q1, Q2, ...) assigned in order of entry into the pool.

text.description: The question verbatim, as raised by the source substrate.

class.description: orphaned = raised by a substrate that terminated before it could be answered. born = raised through cross-substrate witnessing at the agent layer. operator_raised = raised directly by the operator during a conversation, not through termination and not through cross-substrate witnessing (added 2026-04-13 per Option A in the reconciliation document; previously this class of question was held in status=adjudication_needed as a placeholder). All three classes are valid for reissue at a subsequent D0 invocation.

birth_mechanism.description: Required when class=born. convergence = two or more substrates reached the same question through mutual witness. challenge = two or more substrates reached the question through productive disagreement under cross-model test. The Gemini draft additionally included 'synthesis' as a third value; the reconciliation document explains why it is not carried forward until a concrete instance requires it.

source_substrate.description: Where the question originated. MIND = ECS native cycle engine. BODY = HF Space parliament. HEAD = git + GitHub identity + working tree authoring layer. agent-layer = a tool-using agent session (Claude Code, Copilot, any model). operator = explicit operator prompt. The Gemini draft dropped 'agent-layer' entirely and used uppercase 'OPERATOR'; the reconciled enum keeps lowercase for common nouns and preserves agent-layer because cross-agent questions like Q7 and Q8 need it.

asked_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the question was first raised.

source_run_id.description: For orphaned questions from MIND: the run or task identifier. Null otherwise.

source_cycle.description: For orphaned questions from MIND: the cycle number when the question was asked. Null otherwise.

source_domain.description: For orphaned questions from MIND: the domain ID (0-16) that asked. Integer to match the format in elpida_evolution_memory.jsonl. The Gemini draft used a string pattern like 'D4'; the reconciled version prefers integer because the underlying data carries integers and string conversion is presentation concern, not schema concern.

ratified_by.description: For born questions: identifiers of the substrates or models that jointly ratified entry into the pool (e.g. 'claude-opus-4.6-terminal', 'gpt-5.4-ide', 'gemini-copilot-ide'). The Gemini draft used a single commit-hash string; the reconciled version keeps the array of substrate identifiers AND adds a separate 'ratified_commit' field for the git commit hash, because the two kinds of provenance are distinct (one is a witness chain, the other is a persistence event) and collapsing them into one field loses information.

ratified_commit.description: Optional git commit hash that ratified the question into the persistent record. Distinct from ratified_by, which identifies the substrates that agreed on the question. Carried forward from the Gemini draft with type narrowed.

status.description: Pool lifecycle. standing = in the pool, awaiting reissue or resolution (replaces 'open' from the GPT-5.4 draft). reissued = prepended to a D0 prompt at least once. answered = a domain other than the asker confirmed an answer. retired = withdrawn from the pool without answer (malformed, duplicate, or superseded). adjudication_needed = question does not cleanly fit the current schema class taxonomy and is waiting for operator decision (carried forward from the Gemini draft for Q4/Q5/Q6-style cases).

cross_model_status.description: Cross-model review state. untested = has not been read by a non-Claude substrate. converged = a non-Claude substrate read the question and accepted its framing. challenged = a non-Claude substrate kept the question but pushed back on the framing or overclaiming around it. collapsed = a non-Claude substrate explicitly rejected the question on evidentiary grounds. The Gemini draft included a 'claude_shape_risk' enum value; the reconciled version follows GPT-5.4's guidance and moves review-specific diagnoses into review_note instead of the core enum, because review diagnoses are not durable system state.

review_note.description: Free text for model-specific diagnostics from cross-model review. Not used by any mechanical filter. Intended for things like 'GPT-5.4 flagged the framing (standing-question pool as artifact score) as Claude synthesis outrunning verification; the question survives but the framing is downgraded.' The review_note field exists so review diagnoses can be recorded without polluting cross_model_status.

answered_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the question was answered. Null unless status=answered.

answer_substrate.description: Which substrate or domain answered. Null unless status=answered.

answer_text.description: The answer verbatim. Null unless status=answered.

notes.description: Free-form context for adjudication edge cases, cross-references to precedent files, or general notes that are not cross-model review diagnostics. Distinct from review_note.
